metadata.summary: Evaluate, adopt, and use AI plugins in Figma; understand agent workflow boundaries (pre/post-processing only, no headless invocation).

content.title: AI Plugin Ecosystem (Figma)
content.summary: Figma's plugin ecosystem accelerates repetitive design tasks but lacks a stable external API surface. Agents cannot invoke plugins directly — they can only pre-process inputs (generate data files, configuration scripts) and post-process outputs (analyze Figma JSON exports). Understanding which tasks are agent-automatable versus plugin-only prevents wasted engineering effort.

why.p: Figma's plugin ecosystem accelerates repetitive design tasks but lacks a stable external API surface. Agents cannot invoke plugins directly — they can only pre-process inputs (generate data files, configuration scripts) and post-process outputs (analyze Figma JSON exports). Understanding which tasks are agent-automatable versus plugin-only prevents wasted engineering effort.

list.item: Evaluating which Figma AI plugins to adopt for a specific team workflow
Automating repetitive design tasks: bulk renaming, content population, icon generation
Generating a plugin adoption policy with accessibility and brand guardrails
Auditing design files for missing content, broken links, or accessibility violations at scale
Final production asset export — AI plugin outputs require human QA before handoff
Design system creation — plugins assist but cannot own system architecture decisions
Small one-off tasks where plugin setup time exceeds manual effort (fewer than ~20 components)
Contexts where plugin API access to external services raises data privacy concerns (e.g., healthcare data in designs)

section.name: plugin-capabilities
section.p: Top Figma AI plugins, their capabilities, agent-friendliness, and hard limitations.

list.item: Magician — AI-powered icon and image generation; in-plugin only; no headless API; outputs vary per run
Automator — batch operations with AI macros (rename, detach, resize); JS macro runner; no external trigger
Content Reel — AI text and image placeholders from data files; data files can be pre-generated by agent
Stark — accessibility checking (contrast, color blind simulation); paid API for CI contrast checks only
Similayer — smart layer selection by similarity; no API surface
Diagram — AI diagramming inside Figma canvas; no API; AI diagramming in-plugin only

section.name: agent-access-boundary
section.p: Agents cannot invoke Figma plugins headlessly. The Figma plugin sandbox is browser-JS only — no server-accessible API exists.

rule.statement: Agents cannot invoke Figma plugins headlessly. The Figma plugin sandbox is browser-JS only — no server-accessible API exists.
rule.rationale: Figma's plugin architecture runs code in a browser-sandboxed iframe. There is no REST API or CLI for triggering plugins externally. Agents that propose API-driven plugin automation are hallucinating.

rule.statement: Agent role is pre-processing (generate data files, configuration scripts) and post-processing (analyze Figma JSON exports). Humans run plugins in Figma.
rule.rationale: This boundary is firm and determined by the Figma plugin sandbox architecture. Designing workflows that respect this boundary avoids wasted engineering effort.

antipattern.description: Relying on AI plugin outputs (Magician, etc.) for deterministic pipeline results.
antipattern.reason: AI plugin outputs vary with each run; reproducibility is poor and not suitable for deterministic design system workflows.

antipattern.description: Using Stark plugin as the only accessibility validation step.
antipattern.reason: Stark flags contrast issues but cannot audit interaction flow or focus order; it is a partial check only.

antipattern.description: Running Automator batch operations on the production Figma file without testing on a duplicate first.
antipattern.reason: Batch operations at scale can break component relationships; test on a duplicate file to validate behavior before applying to production.

section.name: setup-best-practices
section.p: Setup and governance rules for plugin adoption.

rule.statement: Set brand guidelines (color variables, typography styles) in Figma before running any generative plugin.
rule.rationale: AI plugin outputs anchor to existing styles when present; without them, generative outputs default to generic aesthetics that require rework.

rule.statement: Document which content in a file is AI-generated with a Figma annotation.
rule.rationale: AI-generated content must be distinguishable from human-authored content for future audits, brand reviews, and accessibility checks.

rule.statement: Run Stark contrast audit on every screen before handoff; do not rely on visual inspection alone.
rule.rationale: Contrast issues are invisible to designers; automated checking catches them at scale.

rule.statement: Version-pin plugin usage: note plugin version in design system changelog when a plugin modifies components at scale.
rule.rationale: Plugin behavior and output vary across versions; tracking versions prevents accidental regressions when plugins auto-update.

rule.statement: Validate AI-generated icons (Magician) against brand voice before accepting — they skew toward generic/clip-art.
rule.rationale: AI icon generation produces aesthetically safe but on-brand-ambiguous results; human review is required for brand consistency.

section.name: agent-workflow
section.p: What agents can and cannot do with Figma plugins, and recommended patterns for pre/post-processing.

list.item: Generate plugin configuration scripts and batch operation recipes for a human to run in Figma
Analyze Figma file exports (JSON via REST API) to identify opportunities for plugin automation
Produce plugin evaluation matrices scored against team criteria
Pre-generate Content Reel data files (JSON) that a human imports to the plugin

section.p: Most Figma plugins have no stable public REST API — agents cannot invoke them headlessly; Figma's plugin sandbox is browser-JS only. AI plugin outputs (Magician, etc.) vary with each run — reproducibility is poor; not suitable for deterministic pipelines. Plugins like Content Reel pull live data; network failures silently produce empty states. Accessibility plugins (Stark) flag contrast issues but cannot audit interaction flow or focus order. Plugin ecosystem is fragmented: no standard for how plugins consume or emit design tokens. Figma REST API v2 does not expose plugin state — you cannot query "which plugins are active" programmatically.

list.item: haiku — generating bulk operation recipes, plugin configuration checklists, content data files for Content Reel
sonnet — plugin ecosystem evaluation matrices, accessibility gap analysis from Figma JSON export, adoption policy drafts

antipattern.description: Agents will propose API-driven plugin automation that does not exist — Figma plugin sandbox is browser-JS only, not server-accessible.
antipattern.reason: Persistent LLM hallucination. No REST API or external trigger surface exists for Figma plugins.

antipattern.description: LLM output for "write a Figma plugin" is often stale (Plugin API v2 changes); always validate against https://www.figma.com/plugin-docs/.
antipattern.reason: Figma Plugin API v2 has breaking changes from v1; training data lags reality.

antipattern.description: Accessibility remediation suggestions from agents (color values, ARIA labels) require a human to apply in Figma — no write API for component props.
antipattern.reason: Agents can identify issues but not implement fixes without human-in-loop Figma interaction.

antipattern.description: Agent-generated Content Reel data files may produce culturally biased placeholder names/content; review for i18n/diversity before use.
antipattern.reason: LLM-generated names skew toward generic Western conventions; human review required for inclusive design.

section.name: cli-tools-and-services
section.p: CLI tools and external services for plugin workflow automation and analysis.

list.item: figma-export (community) — Export Figma frames/components to PNG/SVG via REST API (https://github.com/lucasecdb/figma-export)
figma-transformer — Parse Figma JSON into structured component trees (npm i figma-transformer)
figma-api (JS SDK) — Figma REST API v1 wrapper (npm i figma-js)
fig-export-cli — Batch export by node name pattern (https://github.com/jemgold/fig)

section.p: Services: Figma REST API v1 (SaaS, agent-friendly for read/export), Magician (SaaS plugin, browser-sandbox only), Automator (SaaS plugin, JS macro runner), Content Reel (SaaS plugin, data-driven), Stark (SaaS plugin, paid API for CI contrast checks only), Diagram (SaaS plugin, no API).

section.name: prompt-patterns
section.p: Recommended prompt patterns for plugin evaluation and workflow design.

example.title: Plugin Evaluation Matrix
example.p: You are a Figma plugin workflow designer. Given this team context: [team size, design system maturity, accessibility requirements]. Evaluate these plugins: Magician, Automator, Content Reel, Stark. Output: scored matrix (criteria: setup cost, output reliability, accessibility coverage, brand safety) + recommended adoption order.

example.title: Figma File Analysis
example.p: Given this Figma file JSON export (truncated): [paste nodes]. Identify: (1) layers likely missing real content (lorem ipsum, placeholder images), (2) components with no accessibility annotations, (3) frames suitable for batch AI generation. Output as actionable task list.

template.purpose: Generate Content Reel-compatible JSON data file from name/email lists
Prompt for scored plugin adoption matrix
